name: Harris v. Viegelahn
summary: The Court held that post-petition wages belong to the debtor after conversion from Chapter 13 to Chapter 7, emphasizing the Code's fresh-start policy and the distinct treatment of pre- and post-petition property.
significance: The Supreme Court's interpretation of the boundaries of the bankruptcy estate and debtors' rights to post-petition wages, relevant to understanding how the Code treats property acquired after filing.
